introduction: This presentation provides an overview of our refrigeration energy costs per store for the year 2025. The data will aid us in forecasting future energy expenditure and potential cost-saving measures.

subsections.heading: Overview of Lee's Market Energy Consumption
subsections.content:
• Lee's Market implemented an energy management system in 2017
• 20% of the company's total energy usage is dedicated to lighting and electrical appliances
• The company's energy usage has been steadily decreasing since 2017 due to efficiency improvements
• In 2019, Lee's Market launched an initiative to further reduce energy consumption by 15% by 2021
• The energy management system provides detailed reports on energy consumption by department and store location
subsections.justification: This slide provides general information about the company's energy consumption but does not specify refrigeration costs in 2025.

subsections.heading: Detailed Analysis of Refrigeration Energy Cost
subsections.content:
• The refrigeration system at Lee's Market accounts for 40% of the total energy consumption
• In 2020, the company installed energy-efficient refrigeration units in all its stores
• The company has a dedicated team to monitor and maintain the refrigeration units
• Lee's Market has a partnership with a leading refrigeration system manufacturer
• The company frequently conducts audits to identify energy waste in its refrigeration systems
subsections.justification: While this slide discusses the company's refrigeration system, it does not provide the specific energy cost in 2025.

subsections.heading: Comparison with Industry Benchmarks
subsections.content:
• Lee's Market aims to be below the industry average in terms of energy consumption
• In 2019, the company's energy consumption was 10% lower than the industry average
• The company has received several awards for its commitment to energy efficiency
• Lee's Market benchmarks its energy performance against other leading retail companies
• The retail industry's average energy cost per square foot has been steadily increasing over the past 5 years
subsections.justification: This slide provides general comparisons with industry benchmarks but does not provide specific refrigeration energy cost figures for 2025.

conclusion: Our refrigeration energy cost has been under control and compares favorably with industry standards. Going forward, we should continue to monitor and identify opportunities for further energy efficiency improvements.
